title: IMD forecast very heavy rainfall in Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, Arunachal Pradesh & Gujarat - News On AIR
article_text: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast very heavy rainfall over Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, Karaikal, Arunachal Pradesh, and Gujarat during the next 2-3 days. The IMD said another fresh cyclonic circulation is very likely to develop over central parts of the south Bay of Bengal around the 12th of this month. The weather department has also predicted light to moderate rainfall over Northeast, West, and South Peninsular India during the next two days. The weather department has also predicted favourable conditions for the further withdrawal of southwest monsoon from remaining parts of Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh and some parts of Maharashtra and Bihar during the next 2-3 days.

title: IMD forecasts heavy rain to continue to lash Kerala - News On AIR
article_text: In Kerala, the met dept forecasts heavy rain to continue in the coming days. An orange alert has been issued today in Palakkad and Malappuram districts and a yellow alert in Thiruvananthapuram, Kollam, Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Idukki, Kozhikode and Wayanad districts. Due to the possibility of strong winds and bad weather, fisherfolks are warned from venturing into the sea on the Kerala coast. Alert is issued in the hilly regions of the State.
